name: Penpot
tagline: Open-source design + prototyping platform.
description: Free Figma alternative — open-source vector design, prototyping, real-time collaboration. Stack: frontend nginx + backend Clojure + Postgres + Redis. Multi-pod install.
notes: Multi-service: frontend, backend, exporter, postgres, redis. Plan ~1–2 GiB at idle. SMTP needed only if you want email invites.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Penpot** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Assets PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 10Gi; Redis PVC: 1Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Five workloads: `frontend` (nginx, image `penpotapp/frontend:latest`), `backend` (image `penpotapp/backend:latest`), `exporter` (image `penpotapp/exporter:latest`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:15-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `PENPOT_SECRET_KEY` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`), Postgres password.
- Backend env: `PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI=https://{{hostname}}`, `PENPOT_DATABASE_URI=postgresql://postgres:5432/penpot`, `PENPOT_REDIS_URI=redis://redis:6379/0`, `PENPOT_REGISTRATION_ENABLED=true` (lock down after first signup), `PENPOT_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps where the image allows.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only `frontend` ingressed, on port 80.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: penpot`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <frontend|backend|exporter|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness: frontend `httpGet / port=80`, backend `httpGet /readyz port=6060`, postgres `pg_isready`, redis `redis-cli PING`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first.

install.values:
# Sentry homelab values — single-instance, traefik ingress, cert-manager TLS.
# Subchart credentials (postgres/redis/kafka/clickhouse) auto-generate; not exposed.

# --- Sentry admin user (the login you create at install time) ---
user:
  create: true
  email: <FILL_ME_IN>
  password: <FILL_ME_IN>

# Run the relay/snuba consumer + upgrade steps as Helm hooks (first-install init).
asHook: true

system:
  url: "https://{{hostname}}"
  adminEmail: ""

# --- Ingress (chart-native, traefik path style) ---
ingress:
  enabled: true
  regexPathStyle: traefik
  ingressClassName: traefik
  hostname: {{hostname}}
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
  tls:
    - secretName: {{instance}}-tls
      hosts:
        - {{hostname}}

# Chart's bundled nginx reverse proxy not needed — traefik fronts it.
nginx:
  enabled: false

# --- Single-node Kafka (KRaft); turn HA quorum down to one controller ---
kafka:
  controller:
    replicaCount: 1
  provisioning:
    enabled: true
    replicationFactor: 1

# --- ClickHouse: single replica/shard, sized for homelab ---
clickhouse:
  clickhouse:
    replicas: 1
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      enabled: true
      dataPersistentVolume:
        enabled: true
        storage: "30Gi"

# --- Filestore (uploaded artifacts/files) on the main data volume ---
filestore:
  backend: filesystem
  filesystem:
    persistence:
      enabled: true
      size: {{storage}}Gi

# --- Resource trims for a single homelab box ---
sentry:
  web:
    replicas: 1
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 300m
        memory: 700Mi
      limits:
        cpu: "2"
        memory: 2Gi
  worker:
    replicas: 1
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 300m
        memory: 700Mi
      limits:
        cpu: "2"
        memory: 2Gi

snuba:
  api:
    replicas: 1
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 200m
        memory: 500Mi
      limits:
        cpu: "1"
        memory: 1500Mi

relay:
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 200Mi
    limits:
      cpu: "1"
      memory: 1Gi

postgresql:
  enabled: true
  primary:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 250m
        memory: 512Mi
      limits:
        cpu: "2"
        memory: 2Gi

redis:
  enabled: true

memcached:
  enabled: true

secrets.label: Sentry admin email
secrets.description: Login email for the initial Sentry admin user created at install.

secrets.label: Sentry admin password
secrets.description: Password for the initial Sentry admin user.

name: Sentry
tagline: Open-source error tracking and performance monitoring.
description: Self-hosted Sentry — capture exceptions, traces, profiles, and replays across your apps. Heavy multi-service install: web + worker + cron + ingest consumers + Snuba (events) + ClickHouse + Postgres + Redis + Kafka + Zookeeper + Memcached + Symbolicator. Expect ~8 GiB RAM at idle.
notes: Heavy install — Sentry self-hosted normally ships as docker-compose with 20+ containers. Use the official sentry-kubernetes Helm chart if you want a tighter, supported deployment; this wizard generates a flat manifest only and may need hand-tuning per service.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Sentry** ({{instance}}) self-hosted on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; ClickHouse PVC: 30Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads (one Deployment each unless noted): `sentry-web` (image `getsentry/sentry:25.1.0`, runs `sentry run web`), `sentry-worker` (`sentry run worker`), `sentry-cron` (`sentry run cron`), `sentry-ingest` (`sentry run consumer ingest-events`), `snuba-api` (image `getsentry/snuba:25.1.0`), `snuba-consumer` (`snuba consumer --storage errors`), `relay` (image `getsentry/relay:25.1.0`). Plus dependency StatefulSets: `postgres:15-alpine`, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`, `redis:7-alpine`, `apache/kafka:3.9.1` (Kafka in KRaft mode — no ZooKeeper), `memcached:1.6-alpine`.
- Launch the Sentry/Snuba/Relay workloads via `args:` so each image's docker-entrypoint and its `sentry`/`snuba` launcher are preserved — e.g. `args: ["run","web"]`, `args: ["run","worker"]`, `args: ["run","cron"]`, `args: ["run","consumer","ingest-events"]` for getsentry/sentry; `args: ["api"]` and `args: ["consumer","--storage","errors"]` for getsentry/snuba; `args: ["run"]` for getsentry/relay. Do NOT override `command:`.
- Kafka runs as a single-node KRaft broker (image `apache/kafka:3.9.1`, no ZooKeeper). Configure it purely through env (do NOT set command/args): `KAFKA_NODE_ID=1`, `KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller`, `KAFKA_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093`, `KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092`, `KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES=CONTROLLER`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@kafka:9093`, `KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_MIN_ISR=1`, `KAFKA_GROUP_INITIAL_REBALANCE_DELAY_MS=0`, `KAFKA_LOG_DIRS=/var/lib/kafka/data`, and a fixed `CLUSTER_ID` (a stable UUID; add a comment that it must not change for the life of the volume). The Kafka StatefulSet's Service MUST be named `kafka` exposing port 9092 (so `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092` resolves) and 9093 for the controller quorum; mount the Kafka PVC at `/var/lib/kafka/data`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` (50-char `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, initial admin email + password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Env on every sentry-* pod: `SENTRY_REDIS_HOST=redis`, `SENTRY_POSTGRES_HOST=postgres`, `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092`, `SENTRY_DB_NAME=sentry`, `SENTRY_DB_USER=sentry`, `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` from secretKeyRef.
- 1 replica per workload (HA is out of scope), `Recreate` strategy.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Sentry image runs as user 999.
- Services: ClusterIP for everything; only `sentry-web` (port 9000) is ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem. Snuba and Kafka 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/3Gi mem. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi mem. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: sentry`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|cron|ingest|snuba-api|snuba-consumer|relay|postgres|clickhouse|redis|kafka|memcached>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` on every pod spec.
- A `sentry-upgrade` Job (Helm-chart-style) that runs `sentry upgrade --noinput` once before web/worker come up — gate it via initContainers or a top-level Job with appropriate ordering.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=9000`, snuba-api `httpGet /health port=1218`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`, kafka `tcpSocket port=9092`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first. Add a top-level comment block above the secret explaining the upgrade Job + post-install user creation step.

install.values:
# SigNoz ({{instance}}) — OpenTelemetry observability via the official signoz/signoz chart.
# Only the SigNoz UI/API is ingressed; the OTLP ingest ports (4317 gRPC, 4318 HTTP)
# stay internal — expose them via a Tailscale Service if remote ingestion is needed.
signoz:
  replicaCount: 1
  ingress:
    enabled: true
    className: traefik
    annotations:
      cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
    hosts:
      - host: {{hostname}}
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            port: 8080
    tls:
      - secretName: {{instance}}-tls
        hosts:
          - {{hostname}}
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
otelCollector:
  replicaCount: 1
  ingress:
    enabled: false
clickhouse:
  enabled: true
  zookeeper:
    enabled: true
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
    size: {{storage}}Gi
telemetryStoreMigrator:
  enabled: true
postgresql:
  enabled: false
redpanda:
  enabled: false
signoz-otel-gateway:
  enabled: false

name: SigNoz
tagline: Open-source DataDog — APM, metrics, logs, traces.
description: Full-stack OpenTelemetry-based observability — APM, metrics, logs and traces backed by ClickHouse. Installed via its official Helm chart `signoz/signoz`, which bundles the unified SigNoz server (UI + API + Alertmanager), the OpenTelemetry collector, ClickHouse, ZooKeeper and the schema migrator. The obvious pick when you want one tool for APM + metrics + logs.
notes: Heavy: the chart bundles ClickHouse (+ ZooKeeper for coordination) and an OpenTelemetry collector — budget 4GB+ free RAM and watch ClickHouse PVC growth under real traffic. Installed through the official Helm chart rather than raw manifests; only the SigNoz UI/API is ingressed (the OTLP ports 4317/4318 stay internal).
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Helm values file for **SigNoz** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
SigNoz ({{instance}}) — OpenTelemetry-based observability (APM, metrics, logs, traces) backed by ClickHouse. Installed via its official Helm chart `signoz/signoz`, which bundles the unified SigNoz server (UI + API + Alertmanager), the OpenTelemetry collector, ClickHouse, ZooKeeper and the schema migrator.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Helm chart `signoz/signoz`.
- Expose ONLY the SigNoz UI/API: `signoz.ingress.enabled: true`, `signoz.ingress.className: traefik`, `signoz.ingress.hosts: [{host: {{hostname}}, paths: [{path: /, pathType: Prefix}]}]`; `signoz.ingress.annotations` include `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure` and `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`; `signoz.ingress.tls: [{secretName: {{instance}}-tls, hosts: [{{hostname}}]}]`.
- Keep `otelCollector.ingress.enabled: false` — note in a comment that the OTLP ingest ports (4317 gRPC, 4318 HTTP) stay internal and can be exposed via a Tailscale Service if remote ingestion is needed.
- Single-node trim: run one replica of the SigNoz server and the otel-collector; keep the bundled `clickhouse` (with its ZooKeeper) and `telemetryStoreMigrator` enabled (`clickhouse.enabled: true`). Do NOT enable `postgresql`, `redpanda` or `signoz-otel-gateway` — they are not needed for a single-cluster install.
- Persistence: size the ClickHouse PVC at `{{storage}}Gi` on the default storageClass; leave the SigNoz server's small SQLite PVC at the chart default.
- Do NOT override image tags — use the chart's pinned defaults (appVersion v0.126.1).
- If {{nodeName}} is non-empty, set a matching `nodeSelector` on the SigNoz, otel-collector and ClickHouse components.

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml`. SigNoz needs no secrets by default, so omit the ```secrets block unless {{notes}} calls for one. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

install.values:
cloud: "private"

siteUrl: https://{{hostname}}

ingress:
  enabled: true
  type: traefik
  hostname: {{hostname}}
  letsencrypt: false
  nginx:
    enabled: false
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: traefik
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
  secretName: {{instance}}-tls

web:
  replicacount: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 300m
      memory: 512Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 1500m
      memory: 1536Mi

worker:
  replicacount: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 300m
      memory: 512Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 1500m
      memory: 1536Mi

plugins:
  replicacount: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 300m
      memory: 512Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 1000m
      memory: 1Gi

postgresql:
  enabled: true
  persistence:
    enabled: true
    size: 10Gi

redis:
  enabled: true

kafka:
  enabled: true

zookeeper:
  enabled: true

name: PostHog
tagline: Open-source product analytics, feature flags, session replays.
description: All-in-one product analytics: event capture, funnels, retention, A/B tests, feature flags, session replays. ClickHouse for analytics, Postgres for app state, Kafka for ingestion, Redis for caching.
notes: PostHog does NOT officially support Kubernetes self-hosting — upstream only supports the docker-compose "hobby" deploy and archived its Helm chart. This is a community-style manifest modeled on the official compose. Image tags are rolling `sha-…` (no semantic versions), so pin/bump deliberately. Heavy: bundles Postgres, Redis, ClickHouse and a single-node Kafka (KRaft) — budget 6GB+ free RAM.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **PostHog** ({{instance}}) self-hosted on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
PostHog ({{instance}}) — product analytics. NOTE: PostHog does NOT officially support Kubernetes self-hosting (only its docker-compose "hobby" deploy); this is a community-style manifest modeled on the official compose. PostHog publishes only rolling `sha-…`/`latest` image tags (no semantic versions) — pin and bump deliberately.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 10Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `posthog-web` (Deployment, image `posthog/posthog:sha-2f894f9`, `args: ["./bin/docker-server"]`), `posthog-worker` (same image, `args: ["./bin/docker-worker-celery","--with-scheduler"]`), `posthog-plugins` (Deployment, image `posthog/posthog-node:sha-30c8ab7` — the CDP/plugin server now ships as its OWN image; use the image's default command `node nodejs/dist/index.js`, so set NO command and NO args), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:15-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`), `clickhouse` (StatefulSet, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.1-alpine`), `kafka` (StatefulSet, `apache/kafka:3.9.1` in KRaft mode — no ZooKeeper).
- Launch the web/worker pods via `args:` (above), NOT `command:` — the `posthog/posthog` image's docker-entrypoint must stay in place.
- Kafka runs as a single-node KRaft broker (image `apache/kafka:3.9.1`, no ZooKeeper). Configure purely through env (no command/args): `KAFKA_NODE_ID=1`, `KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller`, `KAFKA_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093`, `KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092`, `KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES=CONTROLLER`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@kafka:9093`, `KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_MIN_ISR=1`, `KAFKA_GROUP_INITIAL_REBALANCE_DELAY_MS=0`, `KAFKA_AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS_ENABLE=true` (PostHog creates its topics on demand), `KAFKA_LOG_RETENTION_HOURS=24`, `KAFKA_LOG_DIRS=/var/lib/kafka/data`, and a fixed `CLUSTER_ID` (stable UUID; comment that it must not change). The Kafka Service MUST be named `kafka` exposing 9092 (so `KAFKA_HOSTS=kafka:9092` resolves) and 9093; mount the Kafka PVC at `/var/lib/kafka/data`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SECRET_KEY` (50-char Django key `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
- Env on web/worker/plugins: `DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog@postgres:5432/posthog`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0`, `CLICKHOUSE_HOST=clickhouse`, `CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE=posthog`, `KAFKA_HOSTS=kafka:9092`, `SITE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `DISABLE_SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=False`. Pull `SECRET_KEY`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD` from the secret.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Postgres `runAsUser: 999`; ClickHouse `runAsUser: 101`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only `posthog-web` (port 8000) ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. plugins 300m/1000m, 512Mi/1Gi. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi.
- An init Job (image `posthog/posthog:sha-2f894f9`) that runs `python manage.py migrate && python manage.py migrate_clickhouse && python manage.py run_async_migrations` before web/worker come up.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: posthog`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|plugins|postgres|redis|clickhouse|kafka>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=8000`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`, kafka `tcpSocket port=9092`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first. Include the migrate Job.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 2
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 101
        runAsGroup: 101
        fsGroup: 101
      containers:
        - name: excalidraw
          image: excalidraw/excalidraw:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 80
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 25m
              memory: 32Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 128Mi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 80
            initialDelaySeconds: 3
            periodSeconds: 10
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 80
            initialDelaySeconds: 3
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: nginx-cache
              mountPath: /var/cache/nginx
            - name: nginx-run
              mountPath: /var/run
            - name: tmp
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: nginx-cache
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: nginx-run
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: tmp
          emptyDir: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

name: Excalidraw
tagline: Virtual whiteboard for sketchy hand-drawn diagrams.
description: Frontend-only collaborative whiteboard. Single static-served container, no backend state. Loads instantly, drawings persist in browser localStorage by default. Already deployed in this cluster — this entry is a clean template for a second instance.
notes: Stateless. For multi-user real-time collaboration you'd also deploy excalidraw-room (the WebSocket sync server) — not included here; the default UI works for single-user persistent-in-browser use.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Excalidraw** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- No persistent storage (stateless).
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `excalidraw/excalidraw:latest`, `imagePullPolicy: Always`.
- 2 replicas, `RollingUpdate` (maxSurge 1, maxUnavailable 0) — it's stateless.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 101`, `runAsGroup: 101`, `fsGroup: 101` (nginx user inside the image). Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, drop all caps.
- emptyDir mounts at `/var/cache/nginx`, `/var/run`, `/tmp` so the read-only-root nginx can still write its runtime files.
- Container port 80 (the official image's nginx listens on 80, not 8080).
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 → targetPort 80.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests 25m / 32Mi, limits 200m / 128Mi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet / port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 3.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block. No prose outside the fence.

apiVersion: v1

---

    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: server
spec:

      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres

        app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: twenty
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: twenty
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "twenty", "-d", "twenty"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}

kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:

      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}

  template:
    metadata:

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-migrate
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
spec:

  template:
    metadata:

        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
    spec:

        - name: wait-postgres

            - name: REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET

            - name: LOGIN_TOKEN_SECRET

            - name: FILE_TOKEN_SECRET

    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 3000

name: Twenty
tagline: Open-source Salesforce alternative — modern CRM.
description: Notion-meets-Airtable CRM. Customizable objects, kanban + table views, GraphQL API, REST API. Stack: server (NestJS), worker, frontend, Postgres, Redis.
notes: Single-tenant install. Workspace is created on first login through the web UI.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Twenty CRM** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Server data PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `twenty-server` (Deployment, image `twentycrm/twenty:latest`, runs server + GraphQL on 3000), `twenty-worker` (Deployment, same image, runs `yarn worker:prod`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `APP_SECRET` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET`, `REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET`, `LOGIN_TOKEN_SECRET`, `FILE_TOKEN_SECRET` (each 32-byte base64), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`.
- Env on server + worker: `PG_DATABASE_URL=postgres://twenty@postgres:5432/twenty`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `SERVER_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `FRONT_BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `IS_SIGN_UP_DISABLED=false` (lock down after first user), `STORAGE_TYPE=local`, `STORAGE_LOCAL_PATH=/app/.local-storage`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` on twenty pods.
- Server data PVC mounted at `/app/.local-storage`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `twenty-server` exposes port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: server 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. worker 200m/1000m, 256Mi/1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <server|worker|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- An init Job that runs `yarn database:reset` (or `yarn database:migrate:prod`) before the server pod starts — keep it idempotent.
- Readiness: server `httpGet /healthz port=3000` initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  JWT_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-uploads
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: postiz
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: postiz
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "postiz", "-d", "postiz"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  ports:
    - name: redis
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  serviceName: redis
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: redis:7-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["redis-cli", "PING"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h postgres -U postiz; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: postiz
          image: ghcr.io/gitroomhq/postiz-app:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5000
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgresql://postiz:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/postiz
            - name: REDIS_URL
              value: redis://redis:6379
            - name: JWT_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: JWT_SECRET
            - name: IS_GENERAL
              value: "true"
            - name: MAIN_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: FRONTEND_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}/api
            - name: BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL
              value: http://localhost:3000
            - name: STORAGE_PROVIDER
              value: local
            - name: UPLOAD_DIRECTORY
              value: /uploads
            - name: NEXT_PUBLIC_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY
              value: /uploads
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 3Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 5000
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: uploads
              mountPath: /uploads
      volumes:
        - name: uploads
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-uploads
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 5000

secrets.label: JWT secret
secrets.description: Signing key for Postiz auth sessions/cookies (32-byte hex).

secrets.label: Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database.

name: Postiz
tagline: Open-source social media scheduling.
description: Schedule, cross-post, and queue content across Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Bluesky, Mastodon, Threads, Discord, and more. Stack: app + worker + cron + Postgres + Redis. Already deployed in this cluster — this entry is a clean template for a second instance.
notes: Per-platform OAuth credentials are configured via env vars in the secret. Leave the social-platform secret fields blank and add only the ones you want to integrate.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Postiz** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Uploads PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `postiz-app` (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/gitroomhq/postiz-app:latest`, runs the web + API), `postiz-worker` (Deployment, same image, runs `pnpm run start:prod:workers`), `postiz-cron` (Deployment, same image, runs `pnpm run start:prod:cron`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postiz@postgres:5432/postiz`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `JWT_SECRET` (32-byte `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, plus optional `X_API_KEY`, `LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID`, `LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET`, etc. — leave blank when not using the corresponding platform.
- Env on every postiz-* pod: pull `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` from the secret. Plus `FRONTEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}/api`, `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://postiz-app:3000`, `STORAGE_PROVIDER=local`, `UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `MAIN_URL=https://{{hostname}}`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` for postiz pods.
- Uploads PVC mounted at `/uploads` on app + worker.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `postiz-app` exposes ports 3000 (frontend) and 3001 (backend) — Postiz combines both behind a single nginx layer in the image; ingress points at port 4200 (the bundled router) or whichever the image exposes by default; verify against the upstream Dockerfile.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: app 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. worker + cron each 200m/1000m, 256Mi/1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|worker|cron|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Init Job: `pnpm run prisma-db-push` to migrate the schema before app starts.
- Readiness: app `httpGet / port=4200` initialDelaySeconds 30 (the app boots slowly).

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first.

name: n8n
tagline: Fair-code workflow automation — Zapier alternative.
description: Node-RED-style visual workflow builder with 400+ integrations. SQLite by default (fine for single-replica); switch to Postgres for HA. Webhook receiver + cron triggers + execution worker all in one container.
notes: SQLite single-replica install. For HA + execution queue mode, add Postgres + Redis + a worker Deployment running `n8n worker` — not covered in v1.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- PVC: {{storage}}Gi

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `n8nio/n8n:latest`, 1 replica, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` (image's `node` user).
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/home/node/.n8n`.
- Env: `N8N_HOST={{hostname}}`, `WEBHOOK_URL=https://{{hostname}}/`, `N8N_PROTOCOL=https`, `N8N_PORT=5678`, `GENERIC_TIMEZONE=UTC`, `N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true` (use the new task-runner subsystem), `DB_TYPE=sqlite`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`) — required so credentials persist across pod restarts.
- Container port 5678.

- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests 200m / 256Mi, limits 1 / 1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: n8n`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /healthz port=5678` initialDelaySeconds 10.

            - name: config
              mountPath: /config
            - name: cache
              mountPath: /cache
            - name: media
              mountPath: /media
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: config

        - name: media

spec:

name: Jellyfin
tagline: Free software media server for movies, TV, and music.
description: The volunteer-built media system that streams your movies, shows, music, and photos to any device. No tracking, no premium tier - a fully open alternative to Plex and Emby.
notes: The {{storage}}Gi PVC is for config/metadata only. Mount your media from a separate, larger PVC (or an existing one) at /media. Hardware transcoding needs a /dev/dri device mount and node support - left out by default.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Jellyfin** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Jellyfin ({{instance}}) - open-source media server.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `jellyfin/jellyfin:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate` (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Config PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/config`; an `emptyDir` at `/cache`.
- Media volume: add a second PVC named `{{instance}}-media` (size `<FILL_ME_IN>Gi`, ReadWriteOnce) mounted read-only at `/media`. Add a comment that the user can repoint this at an existing media PVC.
- Container port 8096.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8096.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Env: `JELLYFIN_PublishedServerUrl=https://{{hostname}}`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: jellyfin` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /health port=8096`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

apiVersion: v1

    app.kubernetes.io/name: immich

type: Opaque
stringData:
  DB_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>

spec:

apiVersion: v1

    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:

  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: {{instance}}-postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.3.0
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: immich
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: immich
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: DB_PASSWORD
            - name: POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS
              value: "--data-checksums"
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "immich", "-d", "immich"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}

    app.kubernetes.io/name: immich

    app.kubernetes.io/component: server

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: server
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: server
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: immich
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: server
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.3.0
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h {{instance}}-postgres -U immich; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: immich-server
          image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:release
          ports:
            - containerPort: 2283
          env:
            - name: DB_HOSTNAME
              value: {{instance}}-postgres
            - name: DB_PORT
              value: "5432"
            - name: DB_USERNAME
              value: immich
            - name: DB_DATABASE_NAME
              value: immich
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: DB_PASSWORD
            - name: REDIS_HOSTNAME
              value: {{instance}}-redis
            - name: REDIS_PORT
              value: "6379"
            - name: IMMICH_MACHINE_LEARNING_URL
              value: http://{{instance}}-ml:3003
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 768Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 4Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/server/ping
              port: 2283
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 15
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: library
              mountPath: /usr/src/app/upload
      volumes:
        - name: library
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-library

  name: {{instance}}

  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: immich

    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Database password
secrets.description: Password for the Immich Postgres user (used by both the postgres database and the immich-server connection). App-generated; no external dependency.

name: Immich
tagline: High-performance self-hosted photo and video backup.
description: A Google Photos replacement: automatic mobile backup, ML-powered search and face recognition, albums, and sharing. Multi-service - an API server, a machine-learning service, Redis, and a vector-enabled Postgres.
notes: Heaviest entry in the catalog: needs a vector-enabled Postgres, Redis, the API server, and the machine-learning service. Initial ML model download and library import are CPU/RAM hungry. The {{storage}}Gi PVC is the photo library.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Immich** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Immich ({{instance}}) - self-hosted photo/video backup with ML search.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
This is a MULTI-COMPONENT app. Emit all of the following as one multi-document manifest.
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.3.0`): provides the required vector extensions. Env `POSTGRES_USER=immich`, `POSTGRES_DB=immich`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` from secret `{{instance}}-secrets`. 10Gi PVC at `/var/lib/postgresql/data`. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **Redis** (Deployment, image `redis:7-alpine`): ClusterIP service on 6379. No persistence.
- **immich-server** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:release`): port 2283. Env `DB_HOSTNAME`, `DB_USERNAME=immich`, `DB_DATABASE_NAME=immich`, `DB_PASSWORD` (secret), `REDIS_HOSTNAME`, `IMMICH_MACHINE_LEARNING_URL=http://{{instance}}-ml:3003`. Library PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/usr/src/app/upload`.
- **immich-machine-learning** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:release`): port 3003, service `{{instance}}-ml`. 10Gi PVC at `/cache` for downloaded models.
- Pod security on all: `runAsNonRoot: true` where the image allows; `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Service for the server: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 2283.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. (route to the server service)
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: immich` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`. plus `app.kubernetes.io/component` per service.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe on the server: `httpGet /api/server/ping port=2283`, initialDelaySeconds 30.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:

notes: The {{storage}}Gi PVC holds config + metadata. Mount your audio library from a separate PVC at /audiobooks (and /podcasts).

        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: navidrome
          image: deluan/navidrome:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 4533
          env:
            - name: ND_SCANSCHEDULE
              value: 1h
            - name: ND_LOGLEVEL
              value: info
            - name: ND_BASEURL
              value: ""
            - name: ND_DATAFOLDER
              value: /data
            - name: ND_MUSICFOLDER
              value: /music
            - name: ND_PORT
              value: "4533"
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ping
              port: 4533
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /data
            - name: music
              mountPath: /music
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
        - name: music
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-music
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:

    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app

    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

      targetPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  serviceName: {{instance}}-redis
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: redis:7-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["redis-cli", "PING"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:

  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 8000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: paperless
          image: ghcr.io/paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
          env:
            - name: PAPERLESS_REDIS
              value: redis://{{instance}}-redis:6379
            - name: PAPERLESS_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: PAPERLESS_TIME_ZONE
              value: UTC
            - name: PAPERLESS_OCR_LANGUAGE
              value: eng
            - name: USERMAP_UID
              value: "1000"
            - name: USERMAP_GID
              value: "1000"
            - name: PAPERLESS_SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: PAPERLESS_SECRET_KEY
            - name: PAPERLESS_ADMIN_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: PAPERLESS_ADMIN_USER
            - name: PAPERLESS_ADMIN_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: PAPERLESS_ADMIN_PASSWORD
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 2Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/src/paperless/data
            - name: media
              mountPath: /usr/src/paperless/media
            - name: consume
              mountPath: /usr/src/paperless/consume
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
        - name: media
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-media
        - name: consume
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-consume
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.description: Django signing key for sessions and cookies (random, openssl rand -hex 32).

secrets.label: Admin username

secrets.label: Admin password

name: Paperless-ngx
tagline: Scan, index, and archive your physical documents.
description: Turns your paper mountain into a searchable digital archive: OCR, full-text search, automatic tagging, and correspondents. Community-maintained successor to Paperless. Needs a Redis broker alongside the app.
notes: Ships with a Redis sidecar (required broker) and uses SQLite by default - no separate Postgres in v1. OCR of large batches is CPU-heavy. The {{storage}}Gi PVC holds the document archive + data.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Paperless-ngx** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Paperless-ngx ({{instance}}) - document scanning/OCR archive.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit two workloads in one manifest:
- **Redis** (Deployment, image `redis:7-alpine`): ClusterIP service `{{instance}}-redis` on 6379, no persistence.
- **Paperless** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
  - PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/usr/src/paperless/data`; also mount sub-paths or extra PVCs for `/usr/src/paperless/media` and `/usr/src/paperless/consume`.
  - Env: `PAPERLESS_REDIS=redis://{{instance}}-redis:6379`, `PAPERLESS_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `PAPERLESS_TIME_ZONE=UTC`, `PAPERLESS_OCR_LANGUAGE=eng`, `USERMAP_UID=1000`, `USERMAP_GID=1000`.
  - Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `PAPERLESS_SECRET_KEY=<FILL_ME_IN>`, plus `PAPERLESS_ADMIN_USER` / `PAPERLESS_ADMIN_PASSWORD` for the first superuser.
  - Container port 8000.
- Service for Paperless: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: paperless-ngx` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet / port=8000`, initialDelaySeconds 30 (migrations run on first boot).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

tagline: Professional publishing platform for blogs and newsletters.
description: A modern, headless-capable CMS built for blogs, newsletters, and paid memberships. Clean editor, built-in email newsletters, and membership/subscription support. Runs on SQLite for small sites.
notes: Configured for SQLite to stay single-pod. Production email (member signups, magic links) needs SMTP env (`mail__*`) - add your provider. For larger sites Ghost recommends MySQL.

notes: Needs an LLM backend: point `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` at an in-cluster Ollama service, or set `OPENAI_API_BASE_URL` + key. This entry ships the UI only - it does not deploy Ollama.

metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: woodpecker
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}

  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}

secrets.label: Agent shared secret
secrets.description: Shared secret authenticating the agent to the server's gRPC endpoint. App-generated; any random value works as long as server and agent agree.

secrets.label: Gitea OAuth client ID
secrets.description: Client ID of the OAuth2 application you create on your Gitea forge (Settings -> Applications). Redirect URI is https://<hostname>/authorize.

secrets.label: Gitea OAuth client secret
secrets.description: The client secret paired with the Gitea OAuth client ID above.

secrets.label: Gitea base URL
secrets.description: Base URL of your Gitea instance, e.g. https://gitea.example.com (no trailing path).

secrets.label: Admin username(s)
secrets.description: Comma-separated forge username(s) granted Woodpecker admin, e.g. your Gitea login.

name: Woodpecker CI
tagline: Simple, container-native continuous integration.
description: A lightweight CI engine that runs pipelines defined in your repo. Connects to a Git forge (it pairs naturally with the Gitea entry) and executes each step in a container. Server + agent architecture.
notes: Requires an OAuth application on your forge (e.g. Gitea) for `WOODPECKER_GITEA_CLIENT`/`SECRET`, and a shared `WOODPECKER_AGENT_SECRET`. The agent uses the in-cluster Kubernetes backend and needs its own RBAC.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Woodpecker CI** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Woodpecker CI ({{instance}}) - container-native CI server + agent.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit server + agent in one manifest:
- **Server** (Deployment, image `woodpeckerci/woodpecker-server:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/var/lib/woodpecker` (SQLite).
  - Env: `WOODPECKER_HOST=https://{{hostname}}`, `WOODPECKER_OPEN=false`, `WOODPECKER_GITEA=true`, `WOODPECKER_GITEA_URL=<FILL_ME_IN>`, `WOODPECKER_ADMIN=<your-forge-username>`.
  - Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `WOODPECKER_AGENT_SECRET`, `WOODPECKER_GITEA_CLIENT`, `WOODPECKER_GITEA_SECRET` (all `<FILL_ME_IN>`).
  - Ports: 8000 (HTTP) and 9000 (gRPC). ClusterIP service exposing both.
- **Agent** (Deployment, image `woodpeckerci/woodpecker-agent:latest`, 1 replica):
  - ServiceAccount `{{instance}}-agent` + Role/RoleBinding allowing it to manage pods, PVCs, and secrets in `{{namespace}}` (Kubernetes backend).
  - Env: `WOODPECKER_SERVER={{instance}}-server:9000`, `WOODPECKER_AGENT_SECRET` (from secret), `WOODPECKER_BACKEND=kubernetes`, `WOODPECKER_BACKEND_K8S_NAMESPACE={{namespace}}`.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. (route to the server's 8000 port)
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: woodpecker` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`. plus `app.kubernetes.io/component: server|agent`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe on server: `httpGet /healthz port=8000`, initialDelaySeconds 10.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

name: ntfy
tagline: Send push notifications to your phone via simple HTTP.
description: Publish notifications to your phone or desktop with a single HTTP PUT/POST - no app account, no cloud. Great for cron jobs, alerts, and scripts. Pub/sub over HTTP with topic-based subscriptions.
notes: The official mobile apps default to ntfy.sh; point them at https://{{hostname}}. Lock down with `NTFY_AUTH_DEFAULT_ACCESS=deny-all` + users once it's reachable.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  MINIO_ROOT_USER: <FILL_ME_IN>
  MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: s3
      port: 9000
      targetPort: 9000
    - name: console
      port: 9001
      targetPort: 9001
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: minio
          image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-04-22T22-12-26Z
          # First element is the real `minio` binary (image Cmd is ["minio"]);
          # `server` is a minio subcommand, so naming the binary first is required.
          command: ["minio", "server", "/data", "--console-address", ":9001"]
          env:
            - name: MINIO_ROOT_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MINIO_ROOT_USER
            - name: MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD
          ports:
            - name: s3
              containerPort: 9000
            - name: console
              containerPort: 9001
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /minio/health/live
              port: 9000
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 2Gi
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    # Routes to the web console (9001). To expose the S3 API (9000) externally,
    # add a second host block below pointing at port 9000, e.g. s3.{{hostname}}.
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 9001

secrets.label: Root access key (username)
secrets.description: The MinIO root user / S3 access key you log into the console with and use as the S3 access key ID. Operator-chosen, e.g. a strong unique string.

secrets.label: Root secret key (password)
secrets.description: The MinIO root password / S3 secret key. App-generated strong secret.

name: MinIO
tagline: High-performance S3-compatible object storage.
description: An S3-compatible object store you can run yourself - perfect as a backup target or a storage backend for other self-hosted apps. Includes a web console for buckets, users, and policies.
notes: ARCHIVED UPSTREAM: MinIO's GitHub repo is archived (no longer actively developed). It still works, but pin to a specific final `RELEASE.*` image tag rather than `latest`, and consider a maintained alternative (e.g. Garage) for new deployments. Single-node, single-drive layout (not distributed/erasure-coded HA). The Ingress exposes the web console; the S3 API on port 9000 is ClusterIP-only by default - add a second Ingress host if you need external API access.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **MinIO** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
MinIO ({{instance}}) - S3-compatible object storage (single-node).

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `minio/minio` pinned to a specific `RELEASE.*` tag (the repo is archived - do not use `:latest`), with `command: ["minio", "server", "/data", "--console-address", ":9001"]`, 1 replica, `Recreate`. The first element MUST be the real `minio` binary - `server` is a minio subcommand, not an executable, so `command: ["server", ...]` clobbers the image entrypoint and crashes with 'exec: server: executable file not found in $PATH'.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/data`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `MINIO_ROOT_USER` and `MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`, strong).
- Ports: 9000 (S3 API), 9001 (console).
- Service: ClusterIP exposing 9000 and 9001.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. Route the Ingress to the console port 9001. Add a comment showing how to add a second Ingress host for the 9000 API.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: minio` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /minio/health/live port=9000`, initialDelaySeconds 15.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}

    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:

      storage: 10Gi
---
