    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: server
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: authentik
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: authentik
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "authentik", "-d", "authentik"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis

secrets.label: Authentik secret key

name: Authentik
tagline: Flexible self-hosted identity provider (SSO).
description: An identity provider and SSO platform supporting OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, LDAP, and forward-auth. Put it in front of your other apps for single sign-on. Multi-service: server + worker, backed by Postgres and Redis.
notes: Multi-component (server + worker + Postgres + Redis). After install, complete the bootstrap at https://{{hostname}}/if/flow/initial-setup/. Heavier than most entries.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Authentik** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Authentik ({{instance}}) - SSO / identity provider.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit all components in one manifest:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db `authentik`, user `authentik`, password from secret. 10Gi PVC at `/var/lib/postgresql/data`. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **Redis** (Deployment, image `redis:7-alpine`): ClusterIP service on 6379.
- **authentik-server** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2026.5.2`): set `args: ["server"]` and do NOT set `command`. The image entrypoint is `dumb-init -- /lifecycle/ak`, so passing `server` as an arg runs `ak server`; setting `command: ["server"]` REPLACES the entrypoint and the pod crashes with 'exec: server: executable file not found in $PATH'. port 9000. Media PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/media`.
- **authentik-worker** (Deployment, same image): likewise set `args: ["worker"]` (not `command`) so it runs `ak worker`. no ports.
- Shared env on server+worker: `AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__HOST`, `AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__USER=authentik`, `AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__NAME=authentik`, `AUTHENTIK_REDIS__HOST`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY` and `AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000` where the image allows.
- Service for the server: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 9000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: authentik` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`. plus `app.kubernetes.io/component`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe on server: `httpGet /-/health/ready/ port=9000`, initialDelaySeconds 30.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

name: Homepage
tagline: A modern, fully static, highly customizable dashboard.
description: A fast start page for your self-hosted services: bookmarks, service tiles with live status, and widgets for dozens of apps. Configured with simple YAML files. Optional Kubernetes service discovery.
notes: Recent versions refuse requests unless `HOMEPAGE_ALLOWED_HOSTS` includes your hostname (set below). Config lives in the {{storage}}Gi PVC at /app/config - edit the YAMLs there. Kubernetes widget auto-discovery would need extra RBAC (not included).

- Env: `OG_EXTERNAL_URL=https://{{hostname}}`.
- Container port 6157 (HTTP).
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 6157.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: opengist` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet / port=6157`, initialDelaySeconds 10.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  PROXY_AUTH_TOKEN: <FILL_ME_IN>
  COOKIE_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  DUMMY_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods/log"]
    verbs: ["get", "list"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["persistentvolumeclaims"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["events"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: {{instance}}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: {{instance}}
    namespace: {{namespace}}
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-config
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
data:
  jupyterhub_config.py: |
    import os

    c = get_config()  # noqa

    # --- Spawner: one pod per user via KubeSpawner ---
    c.JupyterHub.spawner_class = "kubespawner.KubeSpawner"
    c.KubeSpawner.namespace = os.environ["POD_NAMESPACE"]
    c.KubeSpawner.service_account = os.environ["SERVICE_ACCOUNT"]
    c.KubeSpawner.image = "quay.io/jupyterhub/k8s-singleuser-sample:4.3.5"
    c.KubeSpawner.start_timeout = 300
    c.KubeSpawner.storage_pvc_ensure = True
    c.KubeSpawner.storage_capacity = "1Gi"
    c.KubeSpawner.pvc_name_template = "claim-{username}"
    c.KubeSpawner.uid = 1000
    c.KubeSpawner.fs_gid = 100

    # --- Hub network ---
    c.JupyterHub.hub_ip = "0.0.0.0"
    c.JupyterHub.hub_connect_ip = os.environ["HUB_SERVICE_HOST"] if "HUB_SERVICE_HOST" in os.environ else "{{instance}}-hub"
    c.JupyterHub.hub_connect_url = "http://{{instance}}-hub:8081"
    c.JupyterHub.bind_url = "http://0.0.0.0:8000"

    # --- Proxy (configurable-http-proxy sidecar) ---
    c.ConfigurableHTTPProxy.should_start = True
    c.ConfigurableHTTPProxy.auth_token = os.environ["CONFIGPROXY_AUTH_TOKEN"]
    c.ConfigurableHTTPProxy.api_url = "http://127.0.0.1:8001"

    # --- Persistence (hub SQLite db) ---
    c.JupyterHub.db_url = "sqlite:///srv/jupyterhub/jupyterhub.sqlite"
    c.JupyterHub.cookie_secret = bytes.fromhex(os.environ["JPY_COOKIE_SECRET"])

    # --- Authentication ---
    # Demo authenticator: any username + the shared DUMMY_PASSWORD.
    # Swap to an OAuth authenticator (oauthenticator.*) for real deployments.
    c.JupyterHub.authenticator_class = "dummy"
    c.DummyAuthenticator.password = os.environ["DUMMY_PASSWORD"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-hub
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
  ports:
    - name: hub
      port: 8081
      targetPort: 8081
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 8000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: {{instance}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: hub
          image: quay.io/jupyterhub/jupyterhub:latest
          command: ["jupyterhub", "-f", "/srv/jupyterhub/config/jupyterhub_config.py"]
          env:
            - name: POD_NAMESPACE
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.namespace
            - name: SERVICE_ACCOUNT
              value: {{instance}}
            - name: CONFIGPROXY_AUTH_TOKEN
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: PROXY_AUTH_TOKEN
            - name: JPY_COOKIE_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: COOKIE_SECRET
            - name: DUMMY_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: DUMMY_PASSWORD
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8000
            - name: hub
              containerPort: 8081
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 2Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /hub/health
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /srv/jupyterhub
            - name: config
              mountPath: /srv/jupyterhub/config
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: {{instance}}-config
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: hub
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Proxy auth token
secrets.description: Shared token between the Hub and its configurable-http-proxy (openssl rand -hex 32).

secrets.label: Cookie secret
secrets.description: Hex key used to sign JupyterHub session cookies (openssl rand -hex 32).

secrets.label: Demo login password
secrets.description: Shared password for the built-in DummyAuthenticator — any username logs in with this password. Swap to an OAuth authenticator for real use.

name: JupyterHub
tagline: Multi-user Jupyter notebooks on Kubernetes.
description: Gives each user their own Jupyter notebook server, spawned on demand as a Kubernetes pod. The backbone for data-science and ML engineering teams who want shared, reproducible compute.
notes: The Hub spawns a fresh pod per user via KubeSpawner, so it needs RBAC to manage pods/PVCs in {{namespace}}. For anything beyond a demo, the Zero-to-JupyterHub Helm chart is the supported path.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **JupyterHub** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
JupyterHub ({{instance}}) - multi-user notebooks via KubeSpawner.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- **ServiceAccount** `{{instance}}` + **Role**/**RoleBinding** in `{{namespace}}` allowing pods, pods/log, PVCs, events, and secrets (KubeSpawner manages per-user pods).
- **ConfigMap** with `jupyterhub_config.py`: `c.JupyterHub.spawner_class='kubespawner.KubeSpawner'`, `c.KubeSpawner.namespace='{{namespace}}'`, a single-user image `quay.io/jupyterhub/k8s-singleuser-sample:latest`, and a dummy/native authenticator (comment: swap to OAuth).
- **Hub** Deployment, image `quay.io/jupyterhub/jupyterhub:latest`, using that ServiceAccount; mount the ConfigMap; PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/srv/jupyterhub` (hub SQLite db).
- Pod security on the hub: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Container port 8000.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: jupyterhub` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /hub/health port=8000`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods", "persistentvolumeclaims", "services", "secrets", "configmaps"]
    verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch", "delete"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods/exec", "pods/log"]
    verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch"]
  - apiGroups: ["apps"]
    resources: ["deployments", "replicasets", "statefulsets"]
    verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch", "delete"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: {{instance}}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: {{instance}}
    namespace: {{namespace}}
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: coder
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: coder
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "coder", "-d", "coder"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 7080
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: {{instance}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h postgres -U coder; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: coder
          image: ghcr.io/coder/coder:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 7080
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: CODER_ACCESS_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: CODER_HTTP_ADDRESS
              value: 0.0.0.0:7080
            - name: CODER_PG_CONNECTION_URL
              value: postgres://coder:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/coder?sslmode=disable
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 2Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 7080
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: coder
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.description: Password for the bundled Coder Postgres database. Also embedded into CODER_PG_CONNECTION_URL via $(POSTGRES_PASSWORD).

name: Coder
tagline: Provision remote development environments on demand.
description: Self-hosted cloud development environments defined as code (Terraform). Engineers get consistent, disposable workspaces with their own tools; admins control templates, resources, and access. Backed by Postgres.
notes: Needs Postgres and broad RBAC to provision workspace pods via its Kubernetes/Terraform provider. After install, create a template before workspaces can launch.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Coder** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Coder ({{instance}}) - remote dev-environment platform.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit all components in one manifest:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `coder`, password from secret. 10Gi PVC at `/var/lib/postgresql/data`. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **ServiceAccount** `{{instance}}` + Role/RoleBinding in `{{namespace}}` to manage pods, deployments, PVCs, and services (workspace provisioning).
- **Coder** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/coder/coder:latest`, using that ServiceAccount):
  - Env: `CODER_ACCESS_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `CODER_HTTP_ADDRESS=0.0.0.0:7080`, `CODER_PG_CONNECTION_URL` (from secret).
  - Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `CODER_PG_CONNECTION_URL` and the Postgres password (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
  - Container port 7080.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Service for Coder: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 7080.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: coder` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /healthz port=7080`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: storage
              mountPath: /verdaccio/storage
      volumes:
        - name: storage
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-storage
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: verdaccio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

name: Verdaccio
tagline: A lightweight private npm proxy registry.
description: Host your own npm packages and cache/proxy the public registry. Zero-config to start, with auth and per-package access rules. Great for private packages and faster, offline-friendly installs.
notes: The {{storage}}Gi PVC stores published packages and the proxy cache. Run `npm adduser --registry https://{{hostname}}` to create the first user.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-db
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-db
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: {{instance}}-db
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: sonar
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: sonar
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "sonar", "-d", "sonar"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 9000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        # Elasticsearch (embedded in SonarQube) requires vm.max_map_count >= 262144.
        # This privileged initContainer sets it on the node; the node must permit privileged pods.
        - name: sysctl
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            runAsNonRoot: false
            runAsUser: 0
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: sonarqube
          image: sonarqube:community
          env:
            - name: SONAR_JDBC_URL
              value: jdbc:postgresql://{{instance}}-db:5432/sonar
            - name: SONAR_JDBC_USERNAME
              value: sonar
            - name: SONAR_JDBC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9000
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 2Gi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 4Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/system/status
              port: 9000
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 15
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /opt/sonarqube/data
              subPath: data
            - name: data
              mountPath: /opt/sonarqube/extensions
              subPath: extensions
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database (user/db 'sonar'); SonarQube uses the same value via SONAR_JDBC_PASSWORD.

name: SonarQube
tagline: Continuous inspection of code quality and security.
description: Static analysis that flags bugs, code smells, security hotspots, and coverage gaps across dozens of languages, with quality gates you can enforce in CI. Community Edition backed by Postgres.
notes: Embeds Elasticsearch, which requires `vm.max_map_count >= 262144` on the node - this template adds a privileged initContainer to set it. RAM-hungry (give it 2Gi+). Uses Postgres, not the default H2.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **SonarQube** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
SonarQube Community ({{instance}}) - code-quality analysis.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit two workloads:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `sonar`, password from secret `{{instance}}-secrets`. 5Gi PVC. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **SonarQube** (Deployment, image `sonarqube:community`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - **initContainer** (privileged, `busybox`) running `sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144`. Add a comment that this needs a node that permits it.
  - Pod security on the main container: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
  - PVC `{{storage}}Gi` split across `/opt/sonarqube/data` and `/opt/sonarqube/extensions` (sub-paths fine).
  - Env: `SONAR_JDBC_URL=jdbc:postgresql://{{instance}}-db:5432/sonar`, `SONAR_JDBC_USERNAME=sonar`, `SONAR_JDBC_PASSWORD` (from secret).
  - Container port 9000.
- Service for SonarQube: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 9000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: sonarqube` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /api/system/status port=9000`, initialDelaySeconds 60.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-auth
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
type: Opaque
stringData:
  htpasswd: <FILL_ME_IN>
  REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: registry
          image: registry:2
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5000
          env:
            - name: REGISTRY_AUTH
              value: htpasswd
            - name: REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM
              value: Registry
            - name: REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH
              value: /auth/htpasswd
            - name: REGISTRY_STORAGE_FILESYSTEM_ROOTDIRECTORY
              value: /var/lib/registry
            - name: REGISTRY_HTTP_ADDR
              value: ":5000"
            - name: REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-auth
                  key: REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET
          readinessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 5000
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/registry
            - name: auth
              mountPath: /auth
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
        - name: auth
          secret:
            secretName: {{instance}}-auth
            items:
              - key: htpasswd
                path: htpasswd
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: registry
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: htpasswd file contents
secrets.description: bcrypt htpasswd entries gating push/pull access. Generate with `htpasswd -Bbn <user> <password>` and paste the full output (one or more `user:$2y$...` lines). Mounted at /auth/htpasswd.

secrets.label: HTTP shared secret
secrets.description: Random secret used to sign state shared between registry replicas/uploads.

name: Docker Registry
tagline: Your own private container image registry.
description: The reference OCI/Docker registry (distribution) for pushing and pulling private container images. Minimal and fast - ideal as an in-cluster registry for your own builds.
notes: Add htpasswd auth before exposing it - an open registry is a serious risk. The {{storage}}Gi PVC stores all image layers; clients require HTTPS, which the Ingress provides.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Docker Registry** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Docker Registry ({{instance}}) - private OCI image registry.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `registry:2`, 1 replica, `Recreate`.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/var/lib/registry`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-auth`: an `htpasswd` file (bcrypt) mounted at `/auth/htpasswd` (`<FILL_ME_IN>` - generate with `htpasswd -Bbn user pass`).
- Env: `REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd`, `REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry`, `REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd`, `REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET` (random, from secret).
- Container port 5000.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 5000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. Add annotation `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares` may need a large-body buffer; comment that pushes need a high `proxy-body-size`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /v2/ port=5000` (401 is OK), or a `tcpSocket: 5000` check; initialDelaySeconds 10.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.values:
expose:
  type: ingress
  tls:
    enabled: true
    certSource: secret
    secret:
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  ingress:
    hosts:
      core: {{hostname}}
    className: traefik
    annotations:
      cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure

    labels:

externalURL: https://{{hostname}}
harborAdminPassword: <FILL_ME_IN>
secretKey: <FILL_ME_IN>
persistence:
  enabled: true
  resourcePolicy: keep
  persistentVolumeClaim:
    registry:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: {{storage}}Gi
    jobservice:
      jobLog:
        accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
        size: 1Gi
    database:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
    redis:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
    trivy:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
  imageChartStorage:
    type: filesystem
    filesystem:
      rootdirectory: /storage
database:
  type: internal
redis:
  type: internal
trivy:
  enabled: true

secrets.label: Harbor admin password
secrets.description: Password for the built-in 'admin' user. Used to log into the Harbor UI and as the registry credential for docker login.

secrets.label: Encryption secret key
secrets.description: Key used by Harbor to encrypt sensitive data at rest. MUST be exactly 16 characters.

name: Harbor
tagline: Enterprise container registry with scanning and RBAC.
description: A CNCF registry that adds vulnerability scanning (Trivy), image signing, replication, project-based RBAC, and a polished UI on top of plain image storage. Multi-component; installed via its Helm chart.
notes: The heaviest entry in the catalog (8+ components: core, portal, registry, jobservice, Trivy, Postgres, Redis). Installed through the official Helm chart rather than raw manifests.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Harbor** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Harbor ({{instance}}) - full-featured container registry.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Helm chart `harbor/harbor`.
- `values.yaml`: `expose.type: ingress`, `expose.ingress.className: traefik`, `expose.ingress.hosts.core: {{hostname}}`, `expose.ingress.annotations` include `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}` and the traefik redirect-https middleware; `expose.tls.enabled: true`, `expose.tls.certSource: secret`, `expose.tls.secret.secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- `externalURL: https://{{hostname}}`. `harborAdminPassword: <FILL_ME_IN>`.
- `persistence.enabled: true` with PVC sizes (registry `{{storage}}Gi`, others sized down). Use the default storageClass.
- Keep the bundled internal Postgres and Redis (`database.type: internal`, `redis.type: internal`) for a single-cluster install.
- Enable Trivy (`trivy.enabled: true`).

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml` (placeholder secrets as `<FILL_ME_IN>`), plus a ```secrets block if any sensitive values are needed. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

name: Adminer
tagline: Full-featured database management in a single file.
description: A tiny, fast web UI for managing MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and more - browse tables, run queries, edit data, import/export. Connects to any reachable database; nothing to persist.
notes: Stateless - no PVC. It can reach any database your cluster can; put it behind auth or restrict access since it talks to your DBs.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Adminer** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Adminer ({{instance}}) - single-file database UI.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `adminer:latest`, 1 replica, `RollingUpdate` (stateless). No PVC.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, drop all caps.
- Container port 8080.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8080.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: adminer` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet / port=8080`, initialDelaySeconds 5.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 8978
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      containers:
        - name: cloudbeaver
          image: dbeaver/cloudbeaver:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8978
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 256Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 8978
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /opt/cloudbeaver/workspace
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cloudbeaver
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

name: CloudBeaver
tagline: Web-based database manager for many engines.
description: The browser version of DBeaver: a rich SQL workbench supporting PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, and more, with saved connections, a data editor, and ER diagrams.
notes: First visit runs a setup wizard to create the admin user and saved connections; the {{storage}}Gi PVC keeps them.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
  JWT_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  SESSION_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY: <FILL_ME_IN>
  MAILER_SMTP_URL: <FILL_ME_IN>
  MAILER_ADDRESS_FROM: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: hoppscotch
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: hoppscotch
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "hoppscotch", "-d", "hoppscotch"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 3000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h postgres -U hoppscotch; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: hoppscotch
          image: hoppscotch/hoppscotch:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgresql://hoppscotch:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/hoppscotch
            - name: VITE_BASE_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: VITE_BACKEND_API_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}/backend
            - name: VITE_BACKEND_GQL_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}/backend/graphql
            - name: VITE_BACKEND_WS_URL
              value: wss://{{hostname}}/backend/graphql
            - name: VITE_ADMIN_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}/admin
            - name: WHITELISTED_ORIGINS
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: REDIRECT_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: VITE_ALLOWED_AUTH_PROVIDERS
              value: EMAIL
            - name: JWT_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: JWT_SECRET
            - name: SESSION_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SESSION_SECRET
            - name: DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY
            - name: MAILER_USE_CUSTOM_CONFIGS
              value: "false"
            - name: MAILER_SMTP_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MAILER_SMTP_URL
            - name: MAILER_ADDRESS_FROM
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MAILER_ADDRESS_FROM
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 2Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database (auto-generated).

secrets.label: JWT secret
secrets.description: Signing key for Hoppscotch auth/session JWTs (auto-generated).

secrets.label: Session secret
secrets.description: Secret used to sign server-side sessions (auto-generated).

secrets.label: Data encryption key
secrets.description: 32-character key Hoppscotch uses to encrypt stored secrets (auto-generated).

secrets.label: SMTP connection URL
secrets.description: Full SMTP URL for sending magic-link login emails, e.g. smtps://user:pass@smtp.example.com:465. Required because the default auth provider is EMAIL.

secrets.label: Mailer from address
secrets.description: The From address used on outgoing login emails, e.g. Hoppscotch <noreply@example.com>.

name: Hoppscotch
tagline: A fast, open-source API development client.
description: A lightweight Postman alternative for building and testing REST, GraphQL, and WebSocket requests, with collections, environments, and team sharing. Self-hosted all-in-one image backed by Postgres.
notes: Uses the all-in-one (AIO) image plus Postgres. Login needs an auth provider (OAuth or email/SMTP) configured via env - email magic-link is the simplest start. Several secrets are required.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Hoppscotch** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Hoppscotch ({{instance}}) - self-hosted API client (AIO).

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit two workloads:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `hoppscotch`, password from secret. 5Gi PVC. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **Hoppscotch AIO** (Deployment, image `hoppscotch/hoppscotch:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - Env: `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://hoppscotch:<pw>@{{instance}}-db:5432/hoppscotch`, `VITE_BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `VITE_BACKEND_API_URL=https://{{hostname}}/backend`, `WHITELISTED_ORIGINS=https://{{hostname}}`, `REDIRECT_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `VITE_ALLOWED_AUTH_PROVIDERS=EMAIL`.
  - Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `JWT_SECRET`, `SESSION_SECRET`, `DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (32 chars), plus SMTP creds (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
  - Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
  - Container port 3000 (the AIO image serves app, `/backend`, and `/admin` on one port via its proxy).
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: hoppscotch` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet / port=3000`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database that stores Metabase's application metadata. Auto-generated; you never type it manually.

name: Metabase
tagline: Ask questions of your data, get answers and dashboards.
description: A friendly BI tool that lets engineers and non-engineers query databases visually or in SQL, then build dashboards and alerts. Connects to your production/analytics databases read-only.
notes: Stores its own metadata in Postgres (the default embedded H2 is not durable - don't use it in production). JVM app: give it 1Gi+. Add your data sources from the UI after first login.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Metabase** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Metabase ({{instance}}) - BI / SQL analytics over your databases.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit two workloads:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `metabase`, password from secret. PVC `{{storage}}Gi`. ClusterIP service on 5432.
- **Metabase** (Deployment, image `metabase/metabase:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - Env: `MB_DB_TYPE=postgres`, `MB_DB_HOST={{instance}}-db`, `MB_DB_PORT=5432`, `MB_DB_DBNAME=metabase`, `MB_DB_USER=metabase`, `MB_DB_PASS` (from secret), `MB_SITE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`.
  - Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 2000`, `fsGroup: 2000`.
  - Container port 3000.
- Service for Metabase: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: metabase` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /api/health port=3000`, initialDelaySeconds 60.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

planeVersion: v1.3.1

ingress:
  enabled: true

    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: traefik

    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure

  traefik:
    maxRequestBodyBytes: 20971520

ssl:

redis:
