name: Plane
tagline: Open-source project management (Jira/Linear alternative).
description: Track issues, cycles (sprints), modules, and roadmaps with a fast, modern UI. A self-hosted alternative to Jira and Linear. Multi-service architecture installed via its Helm chart.
notes: Multi-service (web, space, admin, API, workers + Postgres + Redis + MinIO). Installed through the official Plane Helm chart, which wires the pieces together.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Plane** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Plane ({{instance}}) - self-hosted project management.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Plane Helm chart (`makeplane/plane-ce` / `plane` repo).
- `values.yaml`: set `ingress.enabled: true`, `ingress.ingressClass: traefik`, `ingress.appHost: {{hostname}}`, and TLS via `cert-manager` cluster-issuer `{{clusterIssuer}}` with secret `{{instance}}-tls`.
- Keep the bundled internal Postgres, Redis, and MinIO enabled for a single-cluster install; set their PVC sizes (Postgres + MinIO sum to about `{{storage}}Gi`).
- Set the required secret keys (`SECRET_KEY`, MinIO/Postgres/Redis passwords) to `<FILL_ME_IN>` random values.
- Resources: this is heavy - reflect the requests/limits above across the API + worker pods.

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml` (placeholder secrets as `<FILL_ME_IN>`), plus a ```secrets block if any sensitive values are needed. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  ENCRYPTION_KEY: <FILL_ME_IN>
  AUTH_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-db
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-db
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: {{instance}}-db
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: infisical
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: infisical
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "infisical", "-d", "infisical"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  ports:
    - name: redis
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  serviceName: {{instance}}-redis
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: redis:7-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["redis-cli", "PING"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: infisical
          image: infisical/infisical:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: SITE_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: PORT
              value: "8080"
            - name: TELEMETRY_ENABLED
              value: "false"
            - name: DB_CONNECTION_URI
              value: postgres://infisical:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@{{instance}}-db:5432/infisical
            - name: REDIS_URL
              value: redis://{{instance}}-redis:6379
            - name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: ENCRYPTION_KEY
            - name: AUTH_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: AUTH_SECRET
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/status
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Encryption key
secrets.description: Root key Infisical uses to encrypt all stored secrets (openssl rand -hex 16 = 32 hex chars). LOSING THIS MEANS LOSING ACCESS TO EVERY STORED SECRET - back it up before first boot.

secrets.label: Auth secret
secrets.description: Signing key for auth tokens/sessions (Infisical docs: openssl rand -base64 32). A strong random value is generated for you.

secrets.label: Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database.

name: Infisical
tagline: Open-source secrets and environment-variable manager.
description: A central place for your team's secrets and .env values, with per-environment scoping, versioning, access controls, and SDK/CLI integrations to inject secrets into apps and CI. Backed by Postgres and Redis.
notes: Needs Postgres + Redis. Generate ENCRYPTION_KEY (32 hex chars) and AUTH_SECRET (base64) before first boot - losing ENCRYPTION_KEY means losing access to stored secrets.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Infisical** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Infisical ({{instance}}) - secrets / env-var manager.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit all components:
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `infisical`, password from secret. PVC `{{storage}}Gi`. ClusterIP on 5432.
- **Redis** (Deployment, image `redis:7-alpine`): ClusterIP on 6379.
- **Infisical** (Deployment, image `infisical/infisical:latest`, 1 replica, `Recreate`):
  - Env: `SITE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `DB_CONNECTION_URI=postgres://infisical:<pw>@{{instance}}-db:5432/infisical`, `REDIS_URL=redis://{{instance}}-redis:6379`.
  - Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `ENCRYPTION_KEY` (32 hex), `AUTH_SECRET` (base64), DB password (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
  - Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
  - Container port 8080.
- Service for Infisical: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8080.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: infisical` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /api/status port=8080`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

name: Windmill
tagline: Turn scripts into internal tools, APIs, and workflows.
description: Write scripts in Python, TypeScript, Go, or Bash and Windmill turns them into shareable UIs, scheduled jobs, and composable flows - an open-source platform for internal tooling and automation. Server + workers on Postgres.

installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Windmill** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Windmill ({{instance}}) - script-to-internal-tools platform.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
Emit all components (same image, role chosen by `MODE`):
- **Postgres** (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`): db/user `windmill`, password from secret. PVC `{{storage}}Gi`. ClusterIP on 5432.
- **windmill-server** (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/windmill-labs/windmill:main`): env `MODE=server`, `DATABASE_URL` (from secret), `BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`. Container port 8000.
- **windmill-worker** (Deployment, same image, 1+ replicas): env `MODE=worker`, `DATABASE_URL`, `WORKER_GROUP=default`. No ports.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `DATABASE_URL=postgres://windmill:<pw>@{{instance}}-db:5432/windmill` and the DB password (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Service for the server: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 8000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: windmill` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`. plus `app.kubernetes.io/component: server|worker`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe on server: `httpGet /api/version port=8000`, initialDelaySeconds 15.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  REDMINE_SECRET_KEY_BASE: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 3000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: redmine
          image: redmine:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: REDMINE_SECRET_KEY_BASE
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: REDMINE_SECRET_KEY_BASE
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/src/redmine/files
              subPath: files
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/src/redmine/sqlite
              subPath: sqlite
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: redmine
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Secret key base
secrets.description: Rails signing key used to sign session cookies. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 64`. Required; sessions break if it changes after first login.

name: Redmine
tagline: Classic, flexible project management and issue tracker.
description: A long-standing open-source project manager: issue tracking, Gantt charts, wikis, forums, time tracking, and per-project roles. Battle-tested and runs comfortably on SQLite for small teams.
notes: SQLite single-replica for simplicity. The {{storage}}Gi PVC holds the database and uploaded files/attachments. For larger teams switch to MySQL/Postgres via REDMINE_DB_* env.

install.values:
# Retool ({{instance}}) — low-code internal-tools builder via the official retool/retool chart.
# Multi-service: backend api + jobs-runner + workflows (backend/worker + temporal) +
# code-executor + bundled internal Postgres. Only the backend UI/API is ingressed.
image:
  repository: tryretool/backend
  tag: 3.334.17-stable
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

config:
  # User SSO/license key. Self-hosted free tier works without SSO; SSO + larger teams need a key.
  licenseKey: <FILL_ME_IN>
  # App-generated secrets (treat as openssl-random hex values; do not rotate after first boot).
  encryptionKey: <FILL_ME_IN>
  jwtSecret: <FILL_ME_IN>
  useInsecureCookies: false

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  externalPort: 3000
  internalPort: 3000

ingress:
  enabled: true
  ingressClassName: traefik
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: retool
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: backend
  hosts:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      paths:
        - path: /
  tls:
    - secretName: {{instance}}-tls
      hosts:
        - {{hostname}}

# Bundled internal Postgres (default chart Postgres). Persisted to a PVC.
postgresql:
  enabled: true
  ssl_enabled: false
  auth:
    database: hammerhead_production
    username: postgres
    password: <FILL_ME_IN>
  service:
    port: 5432
  primary:
    persistence:
      enabled: true
      size: {{storage}}Gi

# Workflows (backend + worker + bundled temporal cluster) — enabled by default; keep on.
workflows:
  worker:
    replicaCount: 1
  backend:
    replicaCount: 1

# Code-executor (nsjail-sandboxed code execution) — keep enabled with its default privileged sandbox.
codeExecutor:
  replicaCount: 1

# 1 combined backend+jobs pod is enough for a small self-hosted deployment.
replicaCount: 1

# Shared resources across backend, jobs-runner, dbconnector, and workflows-backend pods.
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 2Gi
  limits:
    cpu: 4000m
    memory: 6Gi

secrets.label: Retool license key
secrets.description: Your Retool self-hosted license key from https://login.retool.com/. The free tier (small teams, no SSO) issues a key too; SSO and larger seat counts require a paid key.

secrets.label: Encryption key
secrets.description: App-generated key used to encrypt resource credentials at rest. Generate once and keep stable; rotating it invalidates stored secrets.

secrets.label: JWT secret
secrets.description: App-generated key used to sign Retool session JWTs.

secrets.label: Internal Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled in-cluster Postgres (user 'postgres', db 'hammerhead_production').

name: Retool
tagline: Build internal tools fast with a drag-and-drop UI builder.
description: A low-code platform for building internal apps over your databases and APIs - drag UI components onto a canvas and wire them to queries. Note: Retool is a commercial product; the self-hosted edition has a limited free tier and requires a license key for SSO and larger teams.
notes: Proprietary (not open-source, unlike the rest of the catalog). Self-hosted is free for small teams; SSO and higher seat counts need a paid license key. Multi-service (backend api + jobs-runner + workflows + code-executor + Postgres) - installed via the official Helm chart.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Retool** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Retool ({{instance}}) - low-code internal-tools builder (commercial, self-hosted).

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Retool Helm chart (`retool/retool`, repo https://github.com/tryretool/retool-helm).
- `values.yaml`: set `config.encryptionKey` and `config.jwtSecret` to `<FILL_ME_IN>` random values; `config.licenseKey` to the user's key (or the free-tier key).
- `config.baseDomain: {{hostname}}`, and enable ingress: `ingress.enabled: true`, `ingress.className: traefik`, annotations for `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}` + the traefik redirect-https middleware, TLS secret `{{instance}}-tls`.
- Keep the bundled internal Postgres enabled (`postgresql.enabled: true`) with a PVC of about `{{storage}}Gi`; set its password to `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Leave the default backend/jobs-runner/workflows/code-executor deployments enabled.
- Resources: heavy - reflect the requests/limits above across the backend + workflow pods.

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml` (placeholder secrets as `<FILL_ME_IN>`), plus a ```secrets block if any sensitive values are needed. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

install.values:
# GitLab ({{instance}}) — cloud-native (gitlab/gitlab) chart on the homelab cluster.
#
# IMPORTANT (chart v10.x): the bundled PostgreSQL, Redis and MinIO subcharts were REMOVED
# upstream. External PostgreSQL, Redis and S3-compatible object storage are now REQUIRED.
# This artifact wires the CLUSTER's Traefik ingress + cert-manager and points GitLab at
# external data services by name. Before installing, the operator must:
#   1. Run an in-cluster PostgreSQL (db "gitlabhq_production", user "gitlab") reachable at
#      {{instance}}-postgresql.{{namespace}}.svc and create Secret {{instance}}-postgresql-password
#      with key "postgresql-password".
#   2. Run an in-cluster Redis reachable at {{instance}}-redis-master.{{namespace}}.svc and create
#      Secret {{instance}}-redis-secret with key "secret".
#   3. Run S3-compatible object storage (e.g. MinIO) and create Secrets
#      {{instance}}-object-storage (key "connection", a Fog/S3 connection YAML) and
#      {{instance}}-registry-storage (key "config", the registry storage stanza).
# The initial root password is auto-generated by the chart; read it after install from the
# Secret {{instance}}-gitlab-initial-root-password (key "password").
global:
  edition: ce
  hosts:
    domain: {{hostname}}
    https: true
    gitlab:
      name: {{hostname}}

  # Use the cluster's Traefik + cert-manager, not the chart's bundled gateway/nginx/cert path.
  gatewayApi:
    enabled: false
    configureCertmanager: false
  ingress:
    enabled: true
    configureCertmanager: false
    provider: traefik
    class: traefik
    tls:
      enabled: true
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
    annotations:
      cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}

  # Kubernetes Agent Server off for a single-node homelab.
  kas:
    enabled: false

  # External PostgreSQL (required since chart v10). Operator-provided.
  psql:
    host: {{instance}}-postgresql.{{namespace}}.svc
    port: 5432
    username: gitlab
    database: gitlabhq_production
    password:
      secret: {{instance}}-postgresql-password
      key: postgresql-password

  # External Redis (required since chart v10). Operator-provided.
  redis:
    host: {{instance}}-redis-master.{{namespace}}.svc
    port: 6379
    auth:
      enabled: true
      secret: {{instance}}-redis-secret
      key: secret

  # External object storage (required since chart v10) via consolidated config.
  appConfig:
    object_store:
      enabled: true
      connection:
        secret: {{instance}}-object-storage
        key: connection
    artifacts:
      bucket: gitlab-artifacts
    lfs:
      bucket: gitlab-lfs
    uploads:
      bucket: gitlab-uploads
    packages:
      bucket: gitlab-packages
    externalDiffs:
      bucket: gitlab-mr-diffs
    terraformState:
      bucket: gitlab-terraform-state
    dependencyProxy:
      bucket: gitlab-dependency-proxy
    backups:
      bucket: gitlab-backups
      tmpBucket: gitlab-tmp

# Do NOT install the chart's bundled cluster infra — use the cluster's own Traefik + cert-manager.
installCertmanager: false
certmanager:
  install: false
# The bundled Let's Encrypt ACME issuer subchart wants an email; disable it since the
# cluster's own cert-manager + ClusterIssuer ({{clusterIssuer}}) issues the cert.
certmanager-issuer:
  install: false
nginx-ingress:
  enabled: false
prometheus:
  install: false
gitlab-runner:
  install: false

# Single-node homelab sizing.
gitlab:
  webservice:
    minReplicas: 1
    maxReplicas: 1
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 1500Mi
  sidekiq:
    minReplicas: 1
    maxReplicas: 1
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 250m
        memory: 1Gi
  gitaly:
    persistence:
      size: {{storage}}Gi
  toolbox:
    replicas: 1
    backups:
      objectStorage:
        config:
          secret: {{instance}}-object-storage
          key: connection
  gitlab-shell:
    minReplicas: 1
    maxReplicas: 1

# Container registry uses external object storage too.
registry:
  enabled: true
  hpa:
    minReplicas: 1
    maxReplicas: 1
  storage:
    secret: {{instance}}-registry-storage
    key: config

name: GitLab CE
tagline: Full DevOps platform: Git, CI/CD, registry, issues.
description: Self-hosted GitLab Community Edition via the all-in-one Omnibus image - Git hosting, merge requests, CI/CD pipelines, a container registry, and issue tracking in a single container with bundled Postgres and Redis. Heavy; best on a node with spare CPU and 8GB+ free memory.
notes: Heaviest single-container entry: GitLab recommends 8 vCPU / 16GB for up to 1,000 users (8GB minimum) and ~40GB+ disk. The bundled Omnibus image runs Postgres, Redis, Gitaly, Sidekiq, Puma and Nginx in one pod, so first boot takes several minutes. Single-replica only; SSH (22) is not exposed by default.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **GitLab CE** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
GitLab Community Edition ({{instance}}) - self-hosted DevOps platform (Git hosting, CI/CD, issues, container registry) via the all-in-one Omnibus image.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Data PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `gitlab/gitlab-ce:17.5.1-ce.0`, 1 replica, Recreate strategy. This is heavy and slow to boot (several minutes on first start).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets` (stringData) with `root-password: "<FILL_ME_IN>"`. Reference it as env `GITLAB_ROOT_PASSWORD` via secretKeyRef - do NOT inline the password anywhere else.
- Env `GITLAB_OMNIBUS_CONFIG` (a multi-line block scalar) sets at least: `external_url 'https://{{hostname}}'`; `gitlab_rails['initial_root_password'] = ENV['GITLAB_ROOT_PASSWORD']`; `nginx['listen_port'] = 80`; `nginx['listen_https'] = false` (TLS terminates at traefik); `gitlab_rails['gitlab_shell_ssh_port'] = 22`. Tune for a small single-node instance: `puma['worker_processes'] = 2`, `sidekiq['max_concurrency'] = 10`, `prometheus_monitoring['enable'] = false`.
- Ports: 80 (HTTP), 22 (SSH).
- PVCs (default storageClass): `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/var/opt/gitlab` (data), plus a 1Gi PVC at `/etc/gitlab` (config).
- Service: ClusterIP exposing 80 and 22 (SSH is not reachable from outside the cluster without a NodePort or LoadBalancer - mention in a comment but do not add one).
- Ingress: port 80 only. `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS: `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: gitlab`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Probes (GitLab boots slowly - be generous): startupProbe `httpGet /-/health` on port 80 with `failureThreshold: 60`, `periodSeconds: 10`; readinessProbe `httpGet /-/readiness` on port 80, `initialDelaySeconds: 60`. Apply the requests/limits above.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document. No prose outside the fence.

name: Zammad
tagline: Open-source helpdesk and ticketing system.
description: Web-based helpdesk / customer-support platform: shared inboxes across email, chat, social and phone, full ticketing with SLAs, a knowledge base, and reporting. Multi-component (Rails app + scheduler + websocket + Elasticsearch + Postgres + Redis + Memcached); installed via its official Helm chart.
notes: Heavy: the chart pulls in Postgres, Elasticsearch, Redis and Memcached as subcharts, and Elasticsearch alone wants ~2GB - budget 4GB+ free RAM. The initial admin account is created through the web UI on first visit (or via the chart's autowizard). Installed through the official Helm chart rather than raw manifests.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Zammad** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Zammad ({{instance}}) - self-hosted helpdesk / ticketing platform.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Helm chart `zammad/zammad`.
- `values.yaml`: `ingress.enabled: true`, `ingress.className: traefik`, `ingress.hosts[0].host: {{hostname}}` with `paths[0].path: /` and `pathType: Prefix`; `ingress.annotations` include `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure` and `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`; `ingress.tls: [{secretName: {{instance}}-tls, hosts: [{{hostname}}]}]`.
- Keep the bundled subcharts enabled for a single-cluster install: `postgresql.enabled: true`, `elasticsearch.enabled: true`, `redis.enabled: true`, `memcached.enabled: true`. Set each datastore password to `<FILL_ME_IN>` (postgresql, redis).
- Persistence: `zammadConfig.persistence.enabled: true` (or the chart's PVC setting) sized at `{{storage}}Gi` on the default storageClass; give Postgres and Elasticsearch their own PVCs sized down from that.
- Trim Elasticsearch for a single node (`elasticsearch.replicas: 1`, modest heap) and run one replica of each Zammad workload.
- Do NOT set an initial admin password in values - the admin account is created through the web UI (autowizard) on first visit.

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml` (placeholder secrets as `<FILL_ME_IN>`), plus a ```secrets block if any sensitive values are needed. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  SECRET_KEY: <FILL_ME_IN>
  UTILS_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_CLIENT_ID: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_AUTH_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_TOKEN_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_USERINFO_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_LOGOUT_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: outline
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: outline
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "outline", "-d", "outline"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  ports:
    - name: redis
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  serviceName: redis
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: redis:7-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["redis-cli", "PING"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-migrate
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
spec:
  backoffLimit: 3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1001
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h postgres -U outline; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: migrate
          image: outlinewiki/outline:latest
          args: ["yarn", "db:migrate"]
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgres://outline:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/outline
            - name: PGSSLMODE
              value: disable
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY
            - name: UTILS_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: UTILS_SECRET
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 3000
      targetPort: 3000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1001
        fsGroup: 1001
      containers:
        - name: outline
          image: outlinewiki/outline:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: PORT
              value: "3000"
            - name: NODE_ENV
              value: production
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgres://outline:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/outline
            - name: PGSSLMODE
              value: disable
            - name: REDIS_URL
              value: redis://redis:6379
            - name: FILE_STORAGE
              value: local
            - name: FILE_STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT_DIR
              value: /var/lib/outline/data
            - name: FILE_STORAGE_UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE
              value: "26214400"
            - name: FORCE_HTTPS
              value: "false"
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY
            - name: UTILS_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: UTILS_SECRET
            - name: OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME
              value: authentik
            - name: OIDC_USERNAME_CLAIM
              value: preferred_username
            - name: OIDC_SCOPES
              value: openid profile email
            - name: OIDC_CLIENT_ID
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_CLIENT_ID
            - name: OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
            - name: OIDC_AUTH_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_AUTH_URI
            - name: OIDC_TOKEN_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_TOKEN_URI
            - name: OIDC_USERINFO_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_USERINFO_URI
            - name: OIDC_LOGOUT_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_LOGOUT_URI
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /_health
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/outline/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 3000

secrets.label: Secret key
secrets.description: Signing key for sessions and cookies (32-byte hex).

secrets.label: Utils secret
secrets.description: Secondary signing key (32-byte hex).

secrets.label: OIDC client ID
secrets.description: From your SSO provider's app registration (Authentik, Google, etc.). Redirect URI is https://<hostname>/auth/oidc.callback.

secrets.label: OIDC client secret
secrets.description: The client secret paired with the client ID above.

secrets.label: OIDC authorize URL
secrets.description: Your provider's authorization endpoint, e.g. https://auth.example.com/application/o/authorize/.

secrets.label: OIDC token URL
secrets.description: Your provider's token endpoint.

secrets.label: OIDC userinfo URL
secrets.description: Your provider's userinfo endpoint.

secrets.label: OIDC logout URL
secrets.description: Your provider's end-session endpoint, e.g. https://auth.example.com/application/o/<app-slug>/end-session/.

name: Outline
tagline: Team knowledge base and wiki.
description: Fast, collaborative team wiki and documentation tool - markdown editing, real-time collaboration, nested collections, full-text search, and a slick reader UI. Stack: app server (Node), Postgres, Redis, plus file storage (local PVC or S3). Requires an external SSO provider for login.
notes: Outline has NO built-in email/password login - it requires an external authentication provider (generic OIDC, Google, Slack, or Azure AD). The self-hosted Authentik already in this catalog can act as the OIDC provider. There is no official Helm chart, so this installs from raw manifests with bundled Postgres + Redis and local file storage.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Outline** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Outline ({{instance}}) - self-hosted team knowledge base / wiki.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Data PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `outline` (Deployment, image `outlinewiki/outline:latest`, listens on 3000), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`). 1 replica each, Recreate.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets` (stringData): `SECRET_KEY` and `UTILS_SECRET` (each a 32-byte hex `<FILL_ME_IN>`, i.e. `openssl rand -hex 32`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`), and the SSO provider credentials `OIDC_CLIENT_ID` + `OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`). Reference everything via secretKeyRef - never inline secrets.
- Env on the app: `URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `PORT=3000`, `NODE_ENV=production`, `DATABASE_URL=postgres://outline:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/outline`, `PGSSLMODE=disable`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `FILE_STORAGE=local`, `FILE_STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT_DIR=/var/lib/outline/data`, `FILE_STORAGE_UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE=26214400`, `FORCE_HTTPS=false` (TLS terminates at traefik).
- Auth (generic OIDC, e.g. pointed at the cluster's Authentik): set `OIDC_AUTH_URI`, `OIDC_TOKEN_URI`, `OIDC_USERINFO_URI`, `OIDC_LOGOUT_URI` and `OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME` as plain env to `<FILL_ME_IN>`; pull `OIDC_CLIENT_ID`/`OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET` from the Secret. Note in a comment that the provider's redirect URI is `https://{{hostname}}/auth/oidc.callback`.
- An init Job (image `outlinewiki/outline:latest`, command `yarn db:migrate`) runs before the app pod to apply migrations; keep it idempotent.
- PVCs (default storageClass): app data `{{storage}}Gi` at `/var/lib/outline/data`, Postgres 5Gi.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, app runs as uid 1001.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `outline` exposes port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: outline`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|postgres|redis>`.
- Resources: app 250m/1000m, 512Mi/1Gi.
- Readiness: app `httpGet /_health port=3000` initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder Secret first. No prose outside the fence.

name: Baserow
tagline: Open-source no-code database, an Airtable alternative.
description: Baserow is an open-source no-code database and Airtable alternative for building databases without writing code. The all-in-one image bundles the backend, web-frontend, an embedded PostgreSQL, Redis, and a Caddy proxy in a single container on port 80, storing all state under /baserow/data. Ideal for keeping structured records — a self-hosted, row-per-item store like a spreadsheet.
notes: The all-in-one baserow/baserow image bundles the backend, web-frontend, embedded PostgreSQL, Redis, and a Caddy proxy in one container on port 80. It intentionally runs as root and is not horizontally scalable (single replica). All state (database, uploads, auto-generated secret/JWT keys) lives under /baserow/data, so the PVC is the durability boundary — no Kubernetes Secret is needed. Create the first admin from the web signup page after install. First boot is slow (embedded Postgres migrations). Core is MIT-licensed; some premium/enterprise features need a paid key. For HA, switch to the standalone images against an external Postgres/Redis.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Baserow ({{instance}}) — open-source no-code database (Airtable alternative). Use the all-in-one `baserow/baserow` image that bundles the backend, web-frontend, embedded PostgreSQL, Redis, and a Caddy proxy on port 80.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Persistent storage: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Single Deployment + Service + Ingress + PersistentVolumeClaim, one YAML document each separated by `---`.
- Image: `baserow/baserow:2.2.2`, `imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent`. Set NO `command:` or `args:` — the image's `/baserow.sh start` entrypoint boots all bundled services.
- 1 replica. Strategy: `Recreate` (PVC is RWO).
- Do NOT set a non-root pod securityContext: the all-in-one image must start as root to launch its embedded PostgreSQL/Redis/Caddy and chown its data dir.
- Env: `BASEROW_PUBLIC_URL=https://{{hostname}}` (drives login URLs and the realtime WebSocket — must be the external https URL). Do NOT set `BASEROW_CADDY_ADDRESSES` (TLS terminates at traefik).
- PVC: `{{storage}}Gi`, default storageClass, mounted at `/baserow/data` (all state — DB, Redis, uploads, generated keys — lives here).
- Container port 80. Service: ClusterIP exposing 80.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests cpu 500m / mem 2Gi, limits cpu 2 / mem 4Gi (bundles many services — do not undersize or it OOMs).
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- Probes on `httpGet path=/api/_health/ port=80`: a startupProbe (periodSeconds 10, failureThreshold 30) to tolerate slow first-boot DB migrations, plus readiness + liveness.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest. No prose outside the fence. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      containers:
        - name: baserow
          image: baserow/baserow:2.2.2
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80
          env:
            - name: BASEROW_PUBLIC_URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 2Gi
            limits:
              cpu: "2"
              memory: 4Gi
          startupProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/_health/
              port: 80
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 30
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/_health/
              port: 80
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/_health/
              port: 80
            periodSeconds: 20
            failureThreshold: 6
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /baserow/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: baserow
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80
